name: Raw meat
description: Raw meat is not toxic to cats per se — cats are obligate carnivores and their digestive systems evolved to handle raw protein. However, commercially available raw meat carries risks from bacterial contamination and, depending on the source, potential parasites. The safety concern is primarily hygiene and contamination, not inherent chemical toxicity.
safetyNotes: Bacterial risk: raw meat (especially chicken, pork, and ground beef) frequently contains Salmonella, Campylobacter, E. coli, or Listeria — can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and systemic illness
Parasitic risk: raw pork and wild game may contain Toxoplasma or Trichinella; raw salmon may carry salmon poisoning disease (Neorickettsia)
Bone risk: cooked bones are dangerous (brittle, splintering); raw bones are generally safer but large fragments can still cause intestinal injury
Seasoned raw meat: any marinade with garlic, onion, salt, or spices is toxic to cats
If feeding raw meat, use commercial raw diets formulated for cats to minimise contamination risk

symptoms.name: Vomiting, Diarrhea, and Abdominal Pain
symptoms.onset: Variable/Acute (typically within hours to a few days depending on the pathogen's incubation period)
symptoms.notes: These symptoms are primarily caused by foodborne pathogens such as Salmonella, E. coli, and Campylobacter, which frequently contaminate raw meat1.... Additionally, if the raw meat includes heavy fat trimmings, the excessive fat can severely irritate the digestive tract and trigger acute pancreatitis

symptoms.name: Gastrointestinal Obstruction or Laceration
symptoms.onset: Immediate to acute (within hours of ingestion).
symptoms.notes: Raw meat diets often include whole or chopped bones. Ingesting bones poses a significant choking hazard, and the bones can splinter to either block the intestinal tract or cause severe lacerations and perforations of the gastrointestinal tissues

symptoms.name: Vitamin A Toxicity (Hypervitaminosis A)
symptoms.onset: Chronic (develops over several weeks to months of repeated consumption)
symptoms.notes: This specific toxicity occurs if a cat is fed a diet rich in raw liver. Chronic consumption of raw liver leads to an overdose of Vitamin A, resulting in a poor coat, constipation, weight loss, painful movements, and excessive/abnormal bone development. symptoms.name: Severe Systemic and Zoonotic Infections
symptoms.onset: Delayed (days to weeks as the infection incubates and spreads systemically).
symptoms.notes: Beyond standard food poisoning bacteria, raw meat can harbor dangerous parasites like Toxoplasma gondii and severe bacterial infections like Mycobacterium bovis (a member of the tuberculosis complex)89.
